perturbation.original_text: WHEREAS, VOTOCAST provides certain hosting and technical services, including iOS and Android Mobile Applications, APIs, SDKs and Admin Module ("Services"); and WHEREAS, Licensee wishes to engage VOTOCAST, and VOTOCAST desires to be engaged by Licensee, to provide hosting and technical services on the terms and subject to the conditions set forth below.
perturbation.changed_text: WHEREAS, VOTOCAST provides certain hosting and technical services, including iOS and Android Mobile Applications, APIs, SDKs and Admin Module ("Platform"); and WHEREAS, Licensee wishes to engage VOTOCAST, and VOTOCAST desires to be engaged by Licensee, to provide hosting and technical services on the terms and subject to the conditions set forth below.
perturbation.explanation: The term "Services" is a key term defined to encompass the hosting and technical services provided by VOTOCAST. Replacing it with "Platform" creates confusion as to whether "Platform" is a subset of "Services" or refers to an entirely different set of obligations. In later sections of the document, where 'Services' is referenced, it becomes unclear whether it includes the components now defined as 'Platform.'
perturbation.contradicted_text:
1.  SERVICES
1.1 Grant of License. Under the terms and conditions of this Agreement, VOTOCAST hereby grants to Licensee a nonexclusive, nontransferable license, to access the Services and provide Licensee's users ("Licensee Users") with access to the Services. Licensee may not transfer the license granted in this Agreement unless such transfer is mutually agreed upon by the Parties in a writing signed by the Parties. VOTOCAST reserves all rights under any intellectual property rights in and to the Services not expressly granted in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from "Services" to "Platform" creates a direct conflict because the original definition of "Services" included specific items like iOS and Android Mobile Applications, APIs, SDKs, and Admin Module. By redefining these as "Platform," the contract now uses the term "Services" without a clear understanding of whether it encompasses "Platform." The contradicted text, which grants a license to access the "Services," becomes ambiguous because it is not clear if this license extends to the components now defined as "Platform." This ambiguity leads to uncertainty and potential disputes regarding the scope of the license and the obligations of VOTOCAST.

perturbation.original_text: 1.3 Acceptance Testing. Prior to the payment of the License and Hosting Fees defined in Exhibit A, Licensee shall have the right to conduct acceptance testing ("Acceptance Testing") to verify that the Services satisfy the acceptance criteria set forth in Exhibit B ("Acceptance Criteria"). Acceptance Testing shall commence within five (5) business days from the date on which VOTOCAST notifies Licensee, in writing by U.S. mail or electronic mail that all implementation work has been completed and is ready for Acceptance Testing by Licensee. In the event that the Services do not conform to the Acceptance Criteria, Licensee shall give VOTOCAST written notice thereof. VOTOCAST shall make commercially reasonable efforts to correct any deficiencies that prevent the Services from conforming to the Acceptance Criteria. When the Services have successfully conformed to or satisfied the Acceptance Criteria, Licensee shall give VOTOCAST written notice by U.S. mail or electronic mail of acceptance ("Acceptance"). Acceptance by Licensee shall be automatically deemed to have occurred: (a) fifteen (15) business days after Licensee's receipt, if Licensee does not communicate to VOTOCAST any defects in the Services, or (b) if Licensee uses the Services in the normal and usual operation of Licensee's business prior to written Acceptance. Licensee shall have the right to terminate this Agreement if, in its sole discretion, the Services have not conformed to the Acceptance Criteria within thirty (30) days from the date of Acceptance Testing; provided however, VOTOCAST shall continue to be entitled to receive, and Licensee shall be obligated to pay to VOTOCAST, any and all amounts owing for Implementation Fees up to the date of termination.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.3 Acceptance Testing. Prior to the payment of the License and Hosting Fees defined in Exhibit A, Licensee shall have the right to conduct acceptance testing ("Acceptance Testing") to verify that the Platform satisfy the acceptance criteria set forth in Exhibit B ("Acceptance Criteria"). Acceptance Testing shall commence within five (5) business days from the date on which VOTOCAST notifies Licensee, in writing by U.S. mail or electronic mail that all implementation work has been completed and is ready for Acceptance Testing by Licensee. In the event that the Platform do not conform to the Acceptance Criteria, Licensee shall give VOTOCAST written notice thereof. VOTOCAST shall make commercially reasonable efforts to correct any deficiencies that prevent the Platform from conforming to the Acceptance Criteria. When the Platform have successfully conformed to or satisfied the Acceptance Criteria, Licensee shall give VOTOCAST written notice by U.S. mail or electronic mail of acceptance ("Acceptance"). Acceptance by Licensee shall be automatically deemed to have occurred: (a) fifteen (15) business days after Licensee's receipt, if Licensee does not communicate to VOTOCAST any defects in the Platform, or (b) if Licensee uses the Platform in the normal and usual operation of Licensee's business prior to written Acceptance. Licensee shall have the right to terminate this Agreement if, in its sole discretion, the Platform have not conformed to the Acceptance Criteria within thirty (30) days from the date of Acceptance Testing; provided however, VOTOCAST shall continue to be entitled to receive, and Licensee shall be obligated to pay to VOTOCAST, any and all amounts owing for Implementation Fees up to the date of termination.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition of acceptance to "Platform", this creates a contradiction due to the confusion regarding "Services" vs "Platform", which ultimately would allow the Licensee to falsely claim the Platform isn't working, since there would be no way to properly test for what isn't properly defined. This leads to uncertainty in the Acceptance Testing and acceptance criteria in Section 1.3 since if the Platform is not defined as the services, there is no reference to the Services.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.4 Additional Services. VOTOCAST and Licensee agree that any additional services ("Additional Services") to be provided by VOTOCAST to Licensee shall be described in a statement of work ("SOW'). which shall be executed by authorized representatives of both Parties. The Parties agree that maintenance and support services as set forth in Sections 2.2 and 2.3, respectively, shall not constitute Additional Services arid shall not require an SOW. Each SOW will be deemed to incorporate the terms and conditions of this Agreement unless SOW explicitly states otherwise. Each SOW shall reference this Agreement, contain a description of the services to be provided and the associated fees and charges, and such additional terms and conditions as the Parties may desire to include. The Services and Additional Services shall be collectively referred to herein as the "Services?' The Agreement and any SOWs shall be collectively referred to herein as the "Agreement." The form of the SOW is outlined in Exhibit D.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Services' for acceptance testing, while the changed text replaces 'Services' with 'Platform'. Section 1.4 then defines 'Services' to include 'Additional Services', creating a contradiction. If acceptance is based on the 'Platform' and 'Services' are defined differently, it's unclear what is being tested and accepted. This directly contradicts the requirement in 1.4 that 'Services' includes 'Additional Services' since the Platform might not include those additional Services. This makes the acceptance criteria vague and potentially unenforceable, fitting the 'Misaligned Terminology' definition and creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 4.1 Services Fees. Licensee shall pay VOTOCAST the Services Fees in accordance with the applicable fee and payment schedule set forth in Exhibit A hereto. VOTOCAST expressly reserves the right to change its rates charged hereunder for the Services during any Renewal Term (as detined herein) but agrees that rates may not increase by more than ten percent (10%) during any Renewal Term. If circumstances require VOTOCAST to raise its rates more than ten percent (10%) during any Renewal Term, VOTOCAST will provide Licensee cost related supporting documentation to justify the rate increase. VOTOCAST will give Licensee at least thirty (30) days prior written notice of any rate changes during any Renewal Term.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.1 Services Fees. Licensee shall pay VOTOCAST the Platform Fees in accordance with the applicable fee and payment schedule set forth in Exhibit A hereto. VOTOCAST expressly reserves the right to change its rates charged hereunder for the Platform during any Renewal Term (as detined herein) but agrees that rates may not increase by more than ten percent (10%) during any Renewal Term. If circumstances require VOTOCAST to raise its rates more than ten percent (10%) during any Renewal Term, VOTOCAST will provide Licensee cost related supporting documentation to justify the rate increase. VOTOCAST will give Licensee at least thirty (30) days prior written notice of any rate changes during any Renewal Term.
perturbation.explanation: By defining Services as Platform in section 4.1, contradicts the original agreement in the preamble. Licensee can now make the argument that other services are being provided that were not intended and are not being paid for due to Services being changed to Platform here. This contradicts Section 1.2 where they should be getting services as set forth in Exhibit A, but this change sets that to be interpreted as only for Platform.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.2 Services. VOTOCAST shall provide the Services to Licensee as specifically set forth in Exhibit A, attached hereto.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Services Fees' with 'Platform Fees' and 'Services' with 'Platform' in Section 4.1. This creates a direct contradiction with Section 1.2, which states that VOTOCAST shall provide the 'Services' as set forth in Exhibit A. By narrowing the scope to 'Platform' only, the changed text implies that VOTOCAST is only obligated to provide services related to the platform, while Section 1.2 originally intended for a broader range of services. This limits Licensee's entitlement and obligations described in Exhibit A, causing a strong in-text contradiction.
